name: Republic of the Congo

outbound_official.note: no official Republic of the Congo government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

visa_free.notes: CEMAC member state. Biometric passport required per Wikipedia citing official sources. Visa exemption ratified by Republic of Congo on 23 October 2017 under CEMAC free movement agreement. Also listed as visa-exempt on official API-Congo (.cg government domain).

Visa exemption confirmed by official Embassy of the Republic of Congo in France (ambacongofr.org) via verbal note to French Ministry of Foreign Affairs dated 27 July 2021. Also listed as visa-exempt on official API-Congo (.cg government domain).

Ordinary passport holders of Rwanda may enter Republic of Congo visa-free for up to 90 days under the Congo-Rwanda Reciprocal Tourist and Business Visa Waiver Agreement, effective September 15, 2023. Agreement covers ordinary, diplomatic, and service passports. The cooperation.gouv.cg (official Congo Ministry of International Cooperation) URL confirming ratification was unreachable during this research session; sourced from Fragomen LLP (immigration law firm) and widely corroborated by international news outlets. Max stay 90 days for ordinary passports.

visa_on_arrival.notes: UAE nationals obtained visa-on-arrival privileges effective October 9, 2018, per Wikipedia citing official sources. No direct official government source URL was accessible during this research session; recorded with low confidence.

visa_policy.notes: The official Embassy of the Republic of Congo in the USA (ambacongo-us.org) explicitly states that 'no Congolese visa will be issued at any port of entry in the Republic of Congo' and that all visa applications must be submitted prior to departure at a Congolese consulate. However, the official API-Congo (.cg government domain) and Wikipedia both document visa-on-arrival for Rwanda (superseded by September 2023 waiver) and UAE (since October 2018). This is a conflict: the official US Embassy statement may be outdated or apply only to nationalities not covered by specific bilateral agreements. VOA for UAE is recorded from Wikipedia (non-official) with low confidence. The official API-Congo source (apicongo.cg — .cg government domain) lists Benin, Burkina Faso, Cameroon, CAR, Côte d'Ivoire, Guinea Conakry, Morocco, Mauritania, Niger, Senegal, Chad, and Togo as visa-exempt, substantially expanding the earlier record which only covered CEMAC + Senegal + Côte d'Ivoire + Benin. President Sassou Nguesso announced on May 25, 2026 (Africa Day, at African Development Bank Annual Meetings in Brazzaville) that all African nationals will gain visa-free entry effective January 1, 2027; this is not yet in force. No official Republic of Congo e-visa portal exists (evisa.gouv.cd is for the Democratic Republic of the Congo, a separate country).

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic or service passports issued by Brazil, China, and Russia do not require a visa to enter Republic of the Congo.
conditional_access.notes: Sourced from Wikipedia citing bilateral agreements. No direct official Congo government page was accessible to confirm these specific agreements during this research session. cooperation.gouv.cg was unreachable.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic passports issued by Portugal and Turkey do not require a visa to enter Republic of the Congo. (Service passport holders from these countries are NOT included.)
conditional_access.notes: Sourced from Wikipedia citing bilateral agreements. No direct official Congo government page was accessible to confirm. cooperation.gouv.cg was unreachable. Angola: a similar agreement for diplomatic and service passports was reportedly signed but not yet in force as of research date.

conditional_access.conditions: Chinese nationals holding passports endorsed 'For Public Affairs' (公务护照, a special Chinese passport category below diplomatic/service but above ordinary) do not require a visa.
conditional_access.notes: Sourced from Wikipedia. Chinese 'Public Affairs' passports are a distinct category. No official Congo government source accessible to confirm.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Republic of the Congo government source.

programs.name: Republic of Congo Residence Permit (Carte de Séjour / Carte de Résident Privilégié)
programs.notes: Residence permits available for 1 year (106,000 FCFA / ~€161.60), 3 years (206,000 FCFA / ~€314.04), or 5 years (106,000 FCFA / ~€161.60), issued by the Direction Départementale de la Surveillance du Territoire. Privileged resident card (carte de résident privilégié) requires uninterrupted residence of at least 10 years. Residence permits are issued automatically to: foreign nationals married for at least 2 years to a Congolese national; economic investors; and leaders of recognised religious denominations. No minimum investment amount stated for the 'economic investor' category on official sources. Source: API-Congo (Investment Promotion Agency) official .cg government domain.

agreements: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) free movement of persons — ratified by Republic of Congo on 23 October 2017
Congo-Rwanda Reciprocal Tourist and Business Visa Waiver Agreement — effective 15 September 2023 (ordinary, diplomatic, service passports; 90 days)
Bilateral visa exemption for diplomatic/service passports of Brazil, China, Russia
Bilateral visa exemption for diplomatic passports of Portugal and Turkey
Pending: Bilateral visa exemption for diplomatic/service passports of Angola (signed, not yet in force)

data_quality.validation_notes: CHANGES vs prior record (June 2026 re-validation): (1) Expanded visa_free list from 4 entries (CEMAC bloc + Senegal + Ivory Coast + Benin) to 15 entries by adding Burkina Faso, Guinea Conakry, Morocco, Mauritania, Niger, Togo (sourced from official API-Congo apicongo.cg), and Rwanda (sourced from Fragomen/news re September 2023 bilateral waiver). (2) Separated CEMAC bloc into 5 individual country entries with iso3 codes and added max_stay_days=90 for CEMAC nations per Wikipedia. (3) Added visa_on_arrival entry for UAE (Wikipedia, non-official source, low confidence) following Wikipedia documentation of an October 2018 arrangement. (4) Populated conditional_access with 3 entries for diplomatic/service/special passport waivers (Brazil, China, Russia for diplomatic+service; Portugal, Turkey for diplomatic only; China 'Public Affairs' passport). These were previously only noted in gaps/notes but not recorded. (5) Added Rwanda to agreements with the September 2023 full visa waiver. (6) Added the 2027 all-African visa-free announcement in the notes (presidential announcement May 25, 2026, Xinhua confirmed, not yet in force). (7) Updated completeness from 'low' to 'medium' given substantial additional entries now recorded.
gaps: Max stay days for most visa-free West African nationalities (Senegal, Benin, Burkina Faso, Guinea Conakry, Morocco, Mauritania, Niger, Togo, Ivory Coast) not specified on official API-Congo source.
Visa on arrival for UAE (October 2018): not confirmed on a directly accessible official Congo government source; recorded from Wikipedia with low confidence.

Rwanda ordinary passport visa-free (September 2023): cooperation.gouv.cg (official Congo MIC) was unreachable during this session; recorded from Fragomen law firm and corroborated by multiple news outlets but not from a directly accessible official .cg source.
Diplomatic/service/special passport waivers (Brazil, China, Russia, Portugal, Turkey): not confirmed on a directly accessible official Congo government source; recorded from Wikipedia only.
Chinese 'Public Affairs' passport exemption: not confirmed on an official source; recorded from Wikipedia only.
Angola diplomatic/service passport agreement: reportedly signed but not confirmed as in-force on any accessible official source.

2027 all-African visa-free policy: announced by President Sassou Nguesso on 25 May 2026 at African Development Bank Annual Meetings (confirmed by Xinhua). Not yet in force; no official decree text accessed.
Congo-Kazakhstan visa exemption memorandum (signed August 2, 2024): scope (diplomatic-only or ordinary) not confirmed on any accessible official source.
Fast-track, skilled worker, digital nomad, or startup visa programs: no official source found.
E-visa or ETA system: no official government e-visa portal found for Republic of the Congo (Congo-Brazzaville). evisa.gouv.cd is for the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
Biometric passport requirement for CEMAC nationals: mentioned on Wikipedia citing official sources but not explicitly stated in the ambacongofr.org announcement text.
The official Congo Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (diplomatie.gouv.cg / diplobrazza.net) provides only general visa overview with no specific nationality exemption list.
RBI economic investor minimum investment threshold: API-Congo lists the category but gives no minimum amount figure.
No Conditional Access for credential-based entry (US/Schengen/UK visa holders): no evidence found on any source.

sources_checked.notes: Official Embassy of the Republic of Congo in France. HTTP 500 during June 2026 re-validation. Previously confirmed CEMAC countries + Senegal + Ivory Coast + Benin visa exemptions as of 27 July 2021 via verbal note to French MFA.

Official API-Congo (Investment Promotion Agency) — official .cg government domain. Successfully fetched June 2026. Lists Benin, Burkina Faso, Cameroon, CAR, Côte d'Ivoire, Guinea Conakry, Morocco, Mauritania, Niger, Senegal, Chad, and Togo as visa-exempt. Also provides residence permit fee schedule.
Official Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (diplomatie.gouv.cg redirects here). Fetched June 2026: states 'most travelers need a visa' but provides no specific nationality exemption list.
Official Embassy of the Republic of Congo to the USA. SSL certificate still expired as of June 2026 (403 Forbidden). States no visa is issued at any port of entry.

Wikipedia. Fetched June 2026. Cites CEMAC (90 days, biometric), UAE VOA (October 2018), Rwanda VOA until 2023 waiver, diplomatic/service passport exemptions for Brazil/China/Russia/Portugal/Turkey, Chinese Public Affairs passports. Used for conditional_access and UAE VOA (with low confidence flag).

Fragomen immigration law firm. Details Congo-Rwanda visa waiver effective September 15, 2023 covering ordinary (90 days), diplomatic, and service passports. Used as primary source for Rwanda visa_free entry given cooperation.gouv.cg unreachable.
Xinhua News Agency. Confirms President Sassou Nguesso announced all-African visa-free entry effective January 1, 2027, at African Development Bank Annual Meetings in Brazzaville on May 25, 2026.
Official Congo Ministry of International Cooperation. ECONNREFUSED during June 2026 re-validation. Would confirm Congo-Rwanda visa exemption ratification.
Official Congo Ministry of Finance. Contains visa/passport fee decrees from 1997 and 2008 but no current comprehensive visa policy page.
